title: Spraguea monosperma
taxonomy: Spraguea monosperma is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It belongs to the genus Spraguea, which is a small group of plants native to the southwestern United States. The plant is further categorized as a dicotyledon, with a typical two-lobed seed leaf. The classification of Spraguea monosperma is also reflected in its botanical name, which follows the binomial nomenclature system. This classification system provides a standardized way to identify and communicate about the plant's characteristics and relationships.

intro: Spraguea monosperma is a small, deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, commonly known as the 'Coyote Brush.' The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throat, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Spraguea monosperma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
